description: Phase 3 — Configuration System: Build the layered configuration system (treb-config crate) that every downstream phase depends on. Supports treb.toml v1/v2, .treb/config.local.json, .env loading, foundry-config integration, namespace resolution, and config validation.

userStories.title: treb-config Crate Scaffold and Config Types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a treb-config workspace crate with all configuration data types (SenderType, SenderConfig, AccountConfig, NamespaceRoles, ForkConfig, TrebFileConfigV2, TrebFileConfigV1, LocalConfig, ResolvedConfig) so that downstream phases have a single source of truth for config structures.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/treb-config/ created with Cargo.toml and src/lib.rs; crate added to workspace members and workspace dependencies
Dependencies added: treb-core, serde, serde_json, toml (0.8), dotenvy (0.15) in workspace deps

SenderType enum with variants PrivateKey, Ledger, Trezor, Safe, OZGovernor and serde string representations (private_key, ledger, trezor, safe, oz_governor); derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash; implements Display and FromStr
SenderConfig struct with type_ (renamed to 'type' in serde), address, private_key, safe, signer, derivation_path, governor, timelock, proposer — all Optional except type_

AccountConfig, NamespaceRoles (profile + senders HashMap), ForkConfig (setup), TrebFileConfigV2 (accounts/namespace/fork with serde defaults), TrebFileConfigV1 (slow + ns HashMap), NamespaceConfigV1, LocalConfig (namespace/network with Default), ResolvedConfig structs all defined
Unit tests for SenderType serde round-trip and Display/FromStr
cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy pass

userStories.title: Local Config Store (.treb/config.local.json)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want read/write support for .treb/config.local.json so that user defaults (namespace, network) can be persisted and loaded, producing identical JSON to the Go version.
acceptanceCriteria: local.rs module with load_local_config(project_root: &Path) -> Result<LocalConfig> that reads .treb/config.local.json, returns defaults if missing, returns TrebError::Config on invalid JSON
save_local_config(project_root: &Path, config: &LocalConfig) -> Result<()> that creates .treb/ dir if needed and writes 2-space indented JSON with trailing newline matching Go format
Round-trip test: save then load asserts equality
Tests for nonexistent file returning defaults, invalid JSON returning descriptive error, and save creating .treb/ directory; all use tempdir
cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy pass

userStories.title: treb.toml V2 Parser
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse the treb.toml v2 format (accounts, namespace, fork sections) with environment variable expansion so that the primary treb config file can be loaded and used.
acceptanceCriteria: trebfile.rs module with load_treb_config_v2(path: &Path) -> Result<TrebFileConfigV2> using toml crate serde deserialization; returns TrebError::Config with file path on parse error or missing file
detect_treb_config_format(project_root: &Path) -> TrebConfigFormat enum (None, V1, V2) that inspects treb.toml content for [accounts]/[namespace]/[fork] vs [ns.] sections
expand_env_vars(s: &str) -> String utility replacing ${VAR_NAME} with env var values (unset vars become empty string); applied to all string fields in AccountConfig/SenderConfig after parsing
Test fixture treb_v2.toml with accounts, namespaces, and fork config; tests for parsing, env var expansion, format detection, and invalid TOML error messages
cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy pass

userStories.title: treb.toml V1 Parser (Legacy Read-Only)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse the legacy treb.toml v1 format with [ns.*] sections so that existing projects using the old format continue to work during migration.
acceptanceCriteria: trebfile_v1.rs module with load_treb_config_v1(path: &Path) -> Result<TrebFileConfigV1> with serde deserialization and env var expansion on sender string fields
convert_v1_to_resolved(v1: &TrebFileConfigV1, namespace: &str) -> ResolvedSenders that merges ns.default with ns.<namespace> (namespace overrides default), extracts profile and slow flag
Test fixture treb_v1.toml with [ns.default] and [ns.live] sections; tests for parsing, merge/override semantics, and slow flag resolution
cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy pass

userStories.title: .env Loading and Foundry Config Integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want .env/.env.local file loading via dotenvy and foundry-config integration so that environment variables and foundry.toml treb-specific senders are available as config sources.
acceptanceCriteria: env.rs module with load_dotenv(project_root: &Path) loading .env then .env.local (local overrides); silently skips missing files; uses dotenvy::from_path_override or equivalent
foundry.rs module with load_foundry_config wrapping foundry_config::Config::load() and extract_treb_senders_from_foundry parsing [profile.*.treb.senders.*] sections, plus rpc_endpoints extraction
Tests: .env loading, .env.local overriding .env, missing .env not erroring, load_foundry_config with valid fixture, extract treb senders from foundry.toml
cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy pass

userStories.title: Namespace Resolution and Layered Config Merger
userStories.description: As a developer, I want namespace resolution (v2 hierarchical dot-walk) and a layered config merger so that all config sources combine into a single ResolvedConfig with deterministic precedence.
acceptanceCriteria: resolver.rs module with resolve_namespace_v2 that walks dot-separated hierarchy (default -> production -> production.ntt), accumulating profile/sender overrides at each level, mapping role names to AccountConfig

resolve_config(opts: ResolveOpts) -> Result<ResolvedConfig> that loads dotenv first, then LocalConfig, detects treb.toml format, loads appropriate version, falls back to foundry.toml senders, applies CLI overrides with highest precedence

Tests: 3-level deep namespace resolution, sender role-to-account mapping with overrides, full resolution with treb.toml v2 + local config + env, fallback to foundry.toml, CLI overrides precedence, missing namespace error listing available namespaces
cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy pass

userStories.title: Config Validation and Error Reporting
userStories.description: As a developer, I want config validation that checks sender cross-references and field requirements so that users get actionable error messages telling them exactly what to fix.
acceptanceCriteria: validation.rs module with validate_config(config: &ResolvedConfig) -> Result<Vec<ConfigWarning>> and validate_sender(name, sender) -> Result<Vec<ConfigWarning>>

Sender validation: private_key requires private_key field; ledger/trezor warn without address/derivation_path; safe requires safe field and signer referencing existing sender; oz_governor requires governor field and proposer referencing existing sender
ConfigWarning struct with message and location fields; error messages include source file, section/key, and actionable fix instructions

Tests: valid config passes, safe with missing signer errors, oz_governor with missing proposer errors, private_key without key errors, ledger without address warns, error messages contain fix instructions
cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy pass
